---
title: "Sergeant Amos Humiston"
---

## Sergeant Amos Humiston

**Dates:** (1830-1863)

### Description

Union soldier killed at the battle of Gettysburg

### Biography

Drew Gilpin Faust, <i>This Republic of Suffering: Death and the American Civil War</i> (New York: Vintage 2008), 11, describes the currency of this story: \'A dead Yankee soldier at Gettysburg was found with an ambrotype of three children \\""tightly clasped in his hands.\\"" The ultimately successful effort to identify him created a sensation, with magazine and newspaper articles, poems, and songs celebrating the devoted father, who perished with his eyes and heart focused on eight-year-old Franklin, six-year-old Alice, and four-year-old Frederick.\'

### Letters

- Letter from May 17th 1871
- Letter from May 10th 1865

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
